recent_coach_change: Barbarez took the job on April 19, 2024 on a four-year deal and guided Bosnia to qualification; no change since.
qualification_path: Qualified via the UEFA play-offs: beat Wales on penalties in the semi-final, then defeated four-time champions Italy on penalties in the play-off final.
key_players: Edin Dzeko (Schalke 04) — captain, age 40, all-time top scorer (72 goals); 6 goals in qualifying including a late playoff equalizer vs Wales
Haris Tabakovic (Borussia Monchengladbach) — striker, 12 Bundesliga goals in 2025-26

injuries_doubts.issue: injury, sat out the June 6 friendly vs Panama

squad_notes: Barbarez named his final 26 on May 11, 2026, headlined by 40-year-old Edin Dzeko. Goalkeeper Osman Hadzikic withdrew injured and was replaced by 18-year-old Mladen Jurkas on June 1. Both Demirovic and Tabakovic made the cut up front.
warmup_assessment: Underwhelming: Bosnia drew 1-1 with Panama in St. Louis on June 6, 2026 (Nikola Katic scoring, Jiovany Ramos equalizing), and were without injured striker Haris Tabakovic.
shape_verdict: A spirited side riding the high of a dramatic playoff run past Wales and Italy, but reliant on a 40-year-old Dzeko and looking less than fluent after a 1-1 draw with Panama.

news.headline: Dzeko headlines Bosnia and Herzegovina's 26-man World Cup squad
Goalkeeper Osman Hadzikic withdraws injured, replaced by 18-year-old Mladen Jurkas
Bosnia and Herzegovina draw 1-1 with Panama in World Cup warm-up
